rows.finalText: For "What is the next right step in my calling", the spread keeps returning to purpose / calling themes with a right path emphasis. Cross (36) in Past sets the tone with a serious theme calls for grounded reflection. Cross often points to the weight of calling, duty, and the difference between a meaningful burden and a draining one in your calling. Present turns on Coffin (8), bringing a cycle closes to make space for renewal to the hinge of the reading and asking for one careful adjustment. Coffin often points to the part of the old path that has already finished. Counterpart (28) in Future suggests consider how the other party experiences this, once the middle-card pressure has been worked with directly. Counterpart colors your calling through the other person's position, their motives, and the part of the story you do not control directly. What ties the vocational pattern together is the link between Cross (36) and Counterpart (28): other with burden, suggesting clarify roles and expectations while staying aware of accepting burdens that are not yours. The spread turns on Coffin (8) more than any other card. Coffin colors your calling through the part of the old path that has already finished, and the rest that is needed before anything genuinely new can take root. Cross (36) with Counterpart (28) keep emphasizing other with burden, suggesting clarify roles and expectations while staying aware of accepting burdens that are not yours. Allow closure and protect recovery time so Counterpart (28) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
